name: Kirk Cameron
subject: Turn America back to Godly values
body:
Please - for the sake of all our families

| | | You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children.
---
| |
---
| | Dear Friend in Faith, Please - for the sake of all our families - take it from me as someone who's lived and worked in Hollywood my whole life... You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children. You may remember shows with wholesome messages like Father Knows Best, Marcus Welby, M.D., and even Dennis the Menace. But today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on _Blue 's Clues_...
---
[](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkc-K2zAQxp9GvilII1m2Dz4k6yQEmlJycOlJ6M84K9aWgi279O2LS_cB9jZ8_GbgN9_Pu76bMIb4_G4mbN0W9LzovM7RTDgHZ6xxHzk9kx__bGbUwKCknFNodo4XvnVeCSiw5ZWSkleKQ_Hegm0YDjVY7hqlTNMMUpWGOQGVQPS-CO1-iXNoODDG6oMyDCzgUA_McVsaItn68ibjcnApLjhvJocNQ4xpn1I8uDQVY_ue82sh4kjgQuCyoFtnPJiIPuWdIHCxs9mQ2pQ-FgIXqHgJDmrqS1RUWilpzVVNsbSVUlgNdcOJuKx50ktaZ4dEdKfuSh_nnoDa4wl9WCciuvP9f-LM9DLhGYnodismOdOn7qpPj2N_1rfr9ReU3_Tj3OsfR_rW3z7XUswYMxHdW3-jn_8o5ta_fodxDIuqiGTPyYTxn2z-ej1bC38DAAD__5e_mII)
---
| | Or Sesame Street using YOUR tax-dollars to normalize same-sex parents and men in dresses. And the result is an upside-down world where children lecture YOU about what you can and cannot say... ...and they try to "educate" YOU about your "old-fashioned" and "mean" ideas. Today you have a very special chance to stop the Hollywood groomers who are turning our children and grandchildren against us and the Godly values we love. **[Today, I'm inviting you to be a Founding Supporter of the very first children's television show that actively fights back against the groomers in Hollywood AND reinforces the traditional biblical values that made America great.](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkc-K2zAQxp9GvilII1m2Dz4k6yQEmlJycOlJ6M84K9aWgi279O2LS_cB9jZ8_GbgN9_Pu76bMIb4_G4mbN0W9LzovM7RTDgHZ6xxHzk9kx__bGbUwKCknFNodo4XvnVeCSiw5ZWSkleKQ_Hegm0YDjVY7hqlTNMMUpWGOQGVQPS-CO1-iXNoODDG6oMyDCzgUA_McVsaItn68ibjcnApLjhvJocNQ4xpn1I8uDQVY_ue82sh4kjgQuCyoFtnPJiIPuWdIHCxs9mQ2pQ-FgIXqHgJDmrqS1RUWilpzVVNsbSVUlgNdcOJuKx50ktaZ4dEdKfuSh_nnoDa4wl9WCciuvP9f-LM9DLhGYnodismOdOn7qpPj2N_1rfr9ReU3_Tj3OsfR_rW3z7XUswYMxHdW3-jn_8o5ta_fodxDIuqiGTPyYTxn2z-ej1bC38DAAD__5e_mII)** Introducing: _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_. I'm making this ground-breaking live-action television show with the great folks at **Brave Books**. If you've seen me and **Brave Books** on _Fox News_ , you know we've already countered those awful "Drag Queen Story Hours" the Left has been pushing on children in our taxpayer funded libraries. Thanks to support from parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens just like you, we started hosting Godly and patriotic "Story Hours" of our own. And now, THOUSANDS of children and parents can gather to pledge allegiance to our flag, read wholesome books, and pray together in their libraries. It's encouraging to see we can win when we fight back! Now we're working to have that same impact in television.
---
>> Imagine a children's show that leaves your child or grandchild with a greater sense of right and wrong and an understanding of truth. Courage, honesty, discernment, forgiveness, empathy, all taught through a biblical and traditional lens. >> Imagine a children's show that brings families together instead of driving them apart. >> Now imagine YOU are the one who makes that show possible. Because we can't beat the Hollywood groomers without your help as a **Founding Supporter**.
---
The great news is that we've already completed and released the first ten character-building episodes of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_! We premiered it recently to a small group of children and parents and I wish you could have seen the smiles on those innocent little faces. And I wish you could have experienced _the parents_ watching their children absorb the beautiful Gospel messages. It was quite a moment for everyone - including folks just like you - who worked so hard to get us that far. But now **Brave Books** has an urgent two-fold problem that I hope you can help with.
---
_**#1. Brave Books needs to launch a massive advertising blitz to get around the groomers on the Left.**_
---
You see, we've made the first five episodes of Season 1 of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ available absolutely FREE. That's the best way we can reach MILLIONS of families with our Gospel-inspired messaging. But no one will see it if we can't get the word out. And the Left will try to bury _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ at every turn. The price tag for an advertising blitz of this magnitude is $250,000 - and with the recent launch, we need to raise the funds immediately.
---
_**#2. We must complete Season 2 to keep our momentum going and that cost is $250,000 for post production.**_
---
I know that's a lot of money but it's actually a fraction of what it would cost if we made our show with a major Hollywood studio. And obviously, we won't work through Hollywood. Hollywood has done quite enough already to destroy American culture and hijack our children. We parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens must take over to rebuild a thriving, healthy, GOOD and GODLY culture. **[That's why I'm asking you to please contribute $35, $50, $250, $500, $2,500 or more to help us reach our fundraising goal of $250,000 for advertising and $250,000 for Season 2.](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkc-K2zAQxp9GvilII1m2Dz4k6yQEmlJycOlJ6M84K9aWgi279O2LS_cB9jZ8_GbgN9_Pu76bMIb4_G4mbN0W9LzovM7RTDgHZ6xxHzk9kx__bGbUwKCknFNodo4XvnVeCSiw5ZWSkleKQ_Hegm0YDjVY7hqlTNMMUpWGOQGVQPS-CO1-iXNoODDG6oMyDCzgUA_McVsaItn68ibjcnApLjhvJocNQ4xpn1I8uDQVY_ue82sh4kjgQuCyoFtnPJiIPuWdIHCxs9mQ2pQ-FgIXqHgJDmrqS1RUWilpzVVNsbSVUlgNdcOJuKx50ktaZ4dEdKfuSh_nnoDa4wl9WCciuvP9f-LM9DLhGYnodismOdOn7qpPj2N_1rfr9ReU3_Tj3OsfR_rW3z7XUswYMxHdW3-jn_8o5ta_fodxDIuqiGTPyYTxn2z-ej1bC38DAAD__5e_mII) ** As our special "Thank You" for becoming a **Founding Supporter** at the $50 level, we'll send you all ten episodes of Season 1 of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ FREE on DVD. This exclusive _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ DVD is only offered to **Founding Supporters**. It won't be available for sale anywhere. So with **[**your gift of $50**](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkc-K2zAQxp9GvilII1m2Dz4k6yQEmlJycOlJ6M84K9aWgi279O2LS_cB9jZ8_GbgN9_Pu76bMIb4_G4mbN0W9LzovM7RTDgHZ6xxHzk9kx__bGbUwKCknFNodo4XvnVeCSiw5ZWSkleKQ_Hegm0YDjVY7hqlTNMMUpWGOQGVQPS-CO1-iXNoODDG6oMyDCzgUA_McVsaItn68ibjcnApLjhvJocNQ4xpn1I8uDQVY_ue82sh4kjgQuCyoFtnPJiIPuWdIHCxs9mQ2pQ-FgIXqHgJDmrqS1RUWilpzVVNsbSVUlgNdcOJuKx50ktaZ4dEdKfuSh_nnoDa4wl9WCciuvP9f-LM9DLhGYnodismOdOn7qpPj2N_1rfr9ReU3_Tj3OsfR_rW3z7XUswYMxHdW3-jn_8o5ta_fodxDIuqiGTPyYTxn2z-ej1bC38DAAD__5e_mII)** , you'll be part of a small select group of people who own our wholesome TV show on DVD. You'll have it ready to watch with the children in your life. Or you can drop your DVD by your local school, church, mission, or even leave it in the waiting room at your doctor's office. So many children have no way of hearing God's message in their lives. Your random act of kindness may change a child's life. **[So please- whether you can send $50, $5,000, $25,000, or anything higher or lower, please respond quickly and know that gifts of EVERY size are needed and greatly appreciated.](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkc-K2zAQxp9GvilII1m2Dz4k6yQEmlJycOlJ6M84K9aWgi279O2LS_cB9jZ8_GbgN9_Pu76bMIb4_G4mbN0W9LzovM7RTDgHZ6xxHzk9kx__bGbUwKCknFNodo4XvnVeCSiw5ZWSkleKQ_Hegm0YDjVY7hqlTNMMUpWGOQGVQPS-CO1-iXNoODDG6oMyDCzgUA_McVsaItn68ibjcnApLjhvJocNQ4xpn1I8uDQVY_ue82sh4kjgQuCyoFtnPJiIPuWdIHCxs9mQ2pQ-FgIXqHgJDmrqS1RUWilpzVVNsbSVUlgNdcOJuKx50ktaZ4dEdKfuSh_nnoDa4wl9WCciuvP9f-LM9DLhGYnodismOdOn7qpPj2N_1rfr9ReU3_Tj3OsfR_rW3z7XUswYMxHdW3-jn_8o5ta_fodxDIuqiGTPyYTxn2z-ej1bC38DAAD__5e_mII)** There's no time to waste. Hollywood groomers have destroyed America's Godly culture. But today we rebuild from the ashes. And it starts by ensuring the most innocent among us are filled with faith and family - not filth. Thank you for being a part of history by becoming a **Founding Supporter** of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_.
---
Sincerely,
---
| | Kirk Cameron (and Iggy!) Actor, Author, and Father

clean_body: Please - for the sake of all our families | | | You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children. | | | | Dear Friend in Faith, Please - for the sake of all our families - take it from me as someone who's lived and worked in Hollywood my whole life... You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children. You may remember shows with wholesome messages like Father Knows Best, Marcus Welby, M.D., and even Dennis the Menace. But today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on Blue 's Clues... | | Or Sesame Street using YOUR tax-dollars to normalize same-sex parents and men in dresses. And the result is an upside-down world where children lecture YOU about what you can and cannot say... ...and they try to "educate" YOU about your "old-fashioned" and "mean" ideas. Today you have a very special chance to stop the Hollywood groomers who are turning our children and grandchildren against us and the Godly values we love. Introducing: Iggy and Mr. Kirk. I'm making this ground-breaking live-action television show with the great folks at Brave Books. If you've seen me and Brave Books on Fox News , you know we've already countered those awful "Drag Queen Story Hours" the Left has been pushing on children in our taxpayer funded libraries. Thanks to support from parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens just like you, we started hosting Godly and patriotic "Story Hours" of our own. And now, THOUSANDS of children and parents can gather to pledge allegiance to our flag, read wholesome books, and pray together in their libraries. It's encouraging to see we can win when we fight back! Now we're working to have that same impact in television. > Imagine a children's show that leaves your child or grandchild with a greater sense of right and wrong and an understanding of truth. Courage, honesty, discernment, forgiveness, empathy, all taught through a biblical and traditional lens. >> Imagine a children's show that brings families together instead of driving them apart. >> Now imagine YOU are the one who makes that show possible. Because we can't beat the Hollywood groomers without your help as a Founding Supporter. The great news is that we've already completed and released the first ten character-building episodes of Iggy and Mr. Kirk! We premiered it recently to a small group of children and parents and I wish you could have seen the smiles on those innocent little faces. And I wish you could have experienced the parents watching their children absorb the beautiful Gospel messages. It was quite a moment for everyone - including folks just like you - who worked so hard to get us that far. But now Brave Books has an urgent two-fold problem that I hope you can help with. #1. Brave Books needs to launch a massive advertising blitz to get around the groomers on the Left. You see, we've made the first five episodes of Season 1 of Iggy and Mr. Kirk available absolutely FREE. That's the best way we can reach MILLIONS of families with our Gospel-inspired messaging. But no one will see it if we can't get the word out. And the Left will try to bury Iggy and Mr. Kirk at every turn. The price tag for an advertising blitz of this magnitude is $250,000 - and with the recent launch, we need to raise the funds immediately. #2. We must complete Season 2 to keep our momentum going and that cost is $250,000 for post production. I know that's a lot of money but it's actually a fraction of what it would cost if we made our show with a major Hollywood studio. And obviously, we won't work through Hollywood. Hollywood has done quite enough already to destroy American culture and hijack our children. We parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens must take over to rebuild a thriving, healthy, GOOD and GODLY culture. As our special "Thank You" for becoming a Founding Supporter at the $50 level, we'll send you all ten episodes of Season 1 of Iggy and Mr. Kirk FREE on DVD. This exclusive Iggy and Mr. Kirk DVD is only offered to Founding Supporters. It won't be available for sale anywhere. So with , you'll be part of a small select group of people who own our wholesome TV show on DVD. You'll have it ready to watch with the children in your life. Or you can drop your DVD by your local school, church, mission, or even leave it in the waiting room at your doctor's office. So many children have no way of hearing God's message in their lives. Your random act of kindness may change a child's life. There's no time to waste. Hollywood groomers have destroyed America's Godly culture. But today we rebuild from the ashes. And it starts by ensuring the most innocent among us are filled with faith and family - not filth. Thank you for being a part of history by becoming a Founding Supporter of Iggy and Mr. Kirk. Sincerely, | | Kirk Cameron (and Iggy!) Actor, Author, and Father | | | Kirk Cameron (and Iggy!) Actor, Author, and Father | | | P.S. The #1 thing you can do today to stop the Hollywood groomers who are tearing families apart - maybe even yours - is to support the very first children's television show in history made possible by contributions from grandparents and seniors like you. Thank you and God bless you and your family! -- Kirk | | Brave Books 13618 Poplar Cir, Suite 302 Conroe, TX 77304 | | Hollywood groomers have destroyed America's Godly culture. But today we rebuild from the ashes. Pop Acta Media | Conservative Innovation PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522 | _

# Saturday, November 29, 2025
---|---
**Good morning!** On this day in 1940, songwriter Francis Rodney Zambon was born in Houston. Known professionally as [Mark James](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOszAQBOCnMV2QvTYGChd_Q5e_vRLZ7JpY54TI3kDy9ifuFa4baaRPmvm6zlefcnqs__2dHL_jNj_oqJmYqczoC9f44lehQJVDYqr-gXmbQUJ3UeoC44w-5c9sGnRB4qgacqq3xqje9rq5OYhodJRB9QEhGIt68FqOxspOB6-HJrkTUwpGBVIpaGMcEBA7rQbbG4vCSMLEW2n5RkxvX2P-bDuVdtnuTXY35mcV-p-AScB0HEcbwnJ2AqZzjIDJF05LpjMuel0P3Pfu-701xeHzSDmnanth5Hr3Kf-q_Lcvdgc_AQAA__-Da3OI), he wrote Elvis Presley's hit single "Suspicious Minds" and won two Grammy awards for Willie Nelson’s rendition of “Always on My Mind.” Today's edition is a little shorter than usual as our staff celebrates the holiday with family and friends. Check out our _Around Texas_ section for the results of the Longhorns-Aggies game, and click [here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDHOozAQRk9jOhAzOHYoXGxDl223RMYewFpjI3sSktuvslf4u0_6pKen9-cxP2yIIW2_7UGG32ueE101EjOV2dvCdX3ys9BClZfAVG3yMc_Y460FaHGcvQ3xM8vGm6X3IzRkQCspQSs9NLtZQQ8O4b6Mg3RrP_Qo3c0pJFLKoR2aYL4wABwBewDs1vXu0fvbAHelpfJC9uQD59LxTkxvW9f4yS8qnctHE83OfFYx_BI4CZyu6-rcXnL6vgKneubCVeDkcoy0kcDJFg4uftdhi6PYFiLfpuD-JntQu4W0UVkKWd8eNrUIoEbUujv3synGn1eIMVSlhey3w4b434N_Vu9l8F8AAAD__6KFhMg) to see what Texas A&M quarterback Marcel Reed's childhood nickname was. If you love Texas history or know someone who does, [TSHA’s extended Black Friday sale](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykzzGO6jAQgOHTJB2RZ2Jip3DxGjpeu6XlZMZgyWBkD4TcfgVX2P7XJ_0_Z38OKaf75X-4sZN3LP7OW8sswtVTqNLiU56VF26yJOEW7pSLR4XHA8ABZ08h5d3rntyiaIaeHZhJazCTGfurU8bQuOBoLcQ4h2U9AqgVKEa76snGPrkPBoAzoALAIUZLSHQcwU5GT9RpxZSk1EGuLPwOLea9vLgOa7n12V1FHq0b_3V46vC0bduQ-RLWvcRv_Kk6PPXV0WNLOac2mU6ryy2k_BXkb98vh78BAAD__1UobUM) is the perfect chance to grab books, maps, and Texas-themed gifts at 25% off all weekend long.
* * *
**Take**** _The Flyover_****with you.** Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your _Flyover_ fix, anywhere.

## DART's Future Uncertain
Dallas Area Rapid Transit (DART), a network of bus routes and light rail lines connecting Dallas and a dozen suburban cities, [could potentially collapse](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGupDAQRE8DmZG7MTYEDjYh-5tuiAxuBksejOwemLn9irnCz0oq6elV_fuZflyIYX_8dU-y_F7TtNNVIjFTnrzLXNYXvzLNVHgOTMXtPqYJJXYCQOAweRfiZ1K1t7P0A9RkwWilwGjT1psdtDZGrjO0kpbWAyqUpkfVttjNPVEd7A0DwAFQAmCzrr1H77sWem2U9pWS5AOn3PBGTG9X1vhJJ-VmSc862o35KFX7p8KxwvG6rmbZctrvtsJxecXbv8LRZQ5LvNOeMm_iixJHdHsSIZ9hf4h7sKB3YHEmpiIQYNBd1zXHdtTZ-uMKMYaiTaXk4-lC_Crw7447Lf4PAAD__7u7gvk) as four member cities have scheduled elections for next year to discontinue funding and withdraw from the network. Established in 1983 [as an expansion](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ8GOhBrjAmFi79Jl7-9Eq3ZdbDkxJG9CeHtT5yU5tqrPu2ONDOar8t8wRDD_fofb2zl7dN8561EFuE8E2Yp_inPzI6LuCBc8E4xzapTQwPQqGkmDHGfdU3WdTRBzRZGozWMZuzr1YIm0GZaAMAjOENMOJgFjRp8v5Crgz3MANQEqgNQrfcnUkRDDyczakOV7piCpNzKysJvLD7u6cW5XdKtjnYVeZSq_1epc6XO27a1R-825WulzujSUz5sDuH3caApnF9h4QYz4-e3hiIp73W29NhCjKGYsdLd9YYh_kTL3wZ7WfUdAAD___blgb8) of the Dallas Transit System, DART is [funded](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQRp_GdI7w8F9QbEN3t90STZgxWDI2soeQvP2KvMLtPx2d7_z7mX_QeRfWv7jzKG8b58BX9izCaSZMku0pZ-InZ3k64YyBfJyhhEYbo2GYCZ3_zHVB47OkwRQ8mq6ta9O1XVVs40A9LbAY01GDPHS9wRaxRzAWzLC0hRtvmDEwGCiNgYe1PQFRU5m-7eqWVF0yOYnpIRsLvzFb_4kvTo8l7oUfN5Ejq-qPgknBdF3XY8ecJWHITnZc75mCaceAK-8cRMF0n1QwNU1VQQOgYJK3vraoD_xkbWPSx_n0btFfzBGToLgYtAs6xCSb_nroLV76jqRd1vYMxKQxkF5vu8BUpJGOy3nvctupulx3dP5rLb9r_RrhfwAAAP__PeWXRA) by a one-cent sales tax collected in member cities, which represents up to 50% of their sales tax revenue since local collections are capped by law at two cents. A 2024 study revealed that some member cities contribute more to DART than the value of the services they receive, sparking calls for reform. After a [failed legislative effort](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2O4jAUxfGncToi3-vgxIWLRSiigC12iykjg6-DNQZH9uXr7UfwCtP_dI70_zpMBxdTvM5_3YUsP0OervSoiZipTN4VruHGt0JHqnyMTNVdfcoTSlyvAFZoJu9iek1d4-1RegMNWeh110Gve9WcLcBAhCCNkUaT6r1RWgZDgaQePHVNtO8xADSAEgDbEAaP3q8VDLrvtBedJB85l5bPxPR0NaRXvlNpT_nSJHtmXqpQfwSOAseTWyLn1H5gO-e7wHETU9rn_H1bBI67WDmXV-vq8hRq3NP8n2oVajuYfwL12wq13W0UDH1TrF8eMaVYdS86OV9cTJ9b_l2su8WfAAAA__9V-X1A) earlier this year, Plano, Irving, Farmers Branch, and Highland Park scheduled withdrawal elections. Although DART [celebrated a major milestone](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOgzAQRL_GdEbsQgwULq6hy7VXIoOXZHUORvYGkr8_kV-4brWjGc2bn-t4dRx4vX27B1l5LXFc6ciBRCiN3iXJy1OeiSbKMrFQdqsPccQKLxpAYz96x-E9NoW3U-V7KMhCa5oGWtPWxd1eyFTQETjvfNdD209owExzYzqsXUUF2zMMAHvACgDLZek8en-poTNtY7xqKvIsMZVyJ6GXy0t4x51SOcdHEexdZMuq_lI4KByO4yjnKZ8Up65w-FgUDudL4fCB0pnDTkkHXknHjdZ8nr-83nSmnVY9szBl7ZdDO05bTKJwKJL128EhcDataqrbw3H4tJD_bbdb_AsAAP__ZoGF0g) in October with the opening of the 26-mile Silver Line connecting Plano and DFW International Airport, three recent shootings have raised questions about its viability as a safe method of transportation. In response, DART allocated nearly $25 million to [improve its security](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHO4yAQBOCnwR0RrAnYhYtr3P3XXmmt2SVBIjiCze8_b3_KvcLVI32amT9f2xfmkuvtNz54kZ90bJXPXliE20bYpKeXvBrv3GXPwh0rlWMDA1dtrYZ5I8zlvbmBlt3QbAdebPDO2eDDONyXKUQmTGMaDfhk0ezkeMKrAWKKPA95-WDWwmzBWAuXlCYCoutoJx-cJ-UMU5ajXeTOwj_YU3kf39wu8XgMZbmLPLsafylYFazneV7OhPgJFazYJMfCCtbHUfmtYP2M0vGo0jCK7k-upMF5_cil5KPqjonlrWNhrCVX7l2_nreGxF3BClPQPO4uzgm0JwraBcN6SjvqeaK42-THYK5DW-h5fsjug3Lm9sBc_hWW_7v5e4G_AQAA__-AMpIh). The withdrawal elections are [scheduled for May](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGysyAUhJ8GOxw5Eg0Fxd_Y5W9v6SAcIjMoDpxI8vZ3zCvcdnd2Z7_9ecwPE2LYn__NhprePs071hKRCPPsTKbiX_TKuGChJRAWs7uYZujgxoXgoGZnQvzMsnF66ZwSDWoxDlKKcRj7ZtVWoTKopLqJ5W7FAKYD2XfjsPSLtVY1QV9lQoAS0AkBrfd3B87denEfRjk4Jjt0gVJuaUXCtyk-ftKJubVpa6JeiY7C-n8MJgZTrbW1S7koLp_B9I0wmC6JwXRB8YxnwMqPaPbEj5yOVEzkKxrHk_f8TISZY0RLIe28BlpdNpX7nDZO2ewlEDdP3O2HwdRk7Y4aYgxlGJnsnpsJ8TuO_nbpqeE3AAD__1RZju4)—just a month before the start of the World Cup, which will feature nine matches at AT&T Stadium in Arlington.
## Best Bites from Around Texas
_Texas Monthly_ [released](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkM9qwzAMh5_GuaVYchInBx9aRg-DDgaFHYNTK43BiUus_sllzz6SXXfb6RNCP0l8X6f2ZH3w0_XDjmT41cd2omcKxExz6-zMqb_zfaaOEneeKdnJhdiixDIHyLFpnfVhaYvMmU66BjIyoKuiAF1plQ0GJFInUdda0UXWVQVYl6ihV7oH2WDmzboMABtACYC7vq8dOlcqqCtdVE4UkpznOO94IKaXTX1Y4oPm3SWOWTAD8y0JtRd4FHj8Y0bg8UluouTsko92ybHO15tbQAm1_xZqz_Riod7Oa1ZgifIUJx7CstWrnd_uOd42lnLD4fC58T36iVM2G3d7-hB8qrQo5HW0Pmxf8v_cPgz-BAAA__-hKoiu) its quadrennial "Top 50 Best BBQ" list in May, but its December issue includes a feature from the magazine's barbecue editor profiling the "best smoked meats he’s eaten" since then. The edition also includes favorite bites from its restaurant critic and taco editor. clean_body: Dallas Area Rapid Transit (DART), a network of bus routes and light rail lines connecting Dallas and a dozen suburban cities, as four member cities have scheduled elections for next year to discontinue funding and withdraw from the network. | | | | | | Saturday, November 29, 2025 ---|--- Good morning! On this day in 1940, songwriter Francis Rodney Zambon was born in Houston. Known professionally as , he wrote Elvis Presley's hit single "Suspicious Minds" and won two Grammy awards for Willie Nelson’s rendition of “Always on My Mind.” Today's edition is a little shorter than usual as our staff celebrates the holiday with family and friends. Check out our Around Texas section for the results of the Longhorns-Aggies game, and click to see what Texas A&M quarterback Marcel Reed's childhood nickname was. If you love Texas history or know someone who does, is the perfect chance to grab books, maps, and Texas-themed gifts at 25% off all weekend long. Take** The Flyoverwith you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | DART's Future Uncertain Dallas Area Rapid Transit (DART), a network of bus routes and light rail lines connecting Dallas and a dozen suburban cities, as four member cities have scheduled elections for next year to discontinue funding and withdraw from the network. Established in 1983 of the Dallas Transit System, DART is by a one-cent sales tax collected in member cities, which represents up to 50% of their sales tax revenue since local collections are capped by law at two cents. A 2024 study revealed that some member cities contribute more to DART than the value of the services they receive, sparking calls for reform. After a earlier this year, Plano, Irving, Farmers Branch, and Highland Park scheduled withdrawal elections. Although DART in October with the opening of the 26-mile Silver Line connecting Plano and DFW International Airport, three recent shootings have raised questions about its viability as a safe method of transportation. In response, DART allocated nearly $25 million to . The withdrawal elections are —just a month before the start of the World Cup, which will feature nine matches at AT&T Stadium in Arlington. Best Bites from Around Texas Texas Monthly its quadrennial "Top 50 Best BBQ" list in May, but its December issue includes a feature from the magazine's barbecue editor profiling the "best smoked meats he’s eaten" since then. The edition also includes favorite bites from its restaurant critic and taco editor. With three locations in Greater Houston, Killen's BBQ sells a that's "heavy on cinnamon and sugar," while Schultze's Pit Room in Seguin offers a Sausage Trio with smoked boudin, jalapeño-cheese, and floral black pepper links that "sing." Houston's Asian American diner has a Bean-offee Pie with a "flair for flavor." It's made with "bananas and a dulce de leche layer with salted and fermented black beans." Lubbock's Jiménez y Friends offers a Pork Belly Taco with Corn and Queso Fresco, described as having "plenty of smoke, tinges of sweetness, and a salty punch of cheese." San Antonio's Gimme Gimme sells a , and the "signature starter" at is Oaxacamole, a guacamole with a "nutty crunch from roasted grasshoppers." | Attention, Texas History lovers! name: Elise Stefanik
subject: Kathy Hochul: “This Bullsh*t has to stop”
body:
Help me defeat the WORST governor in America

| | | | [](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byE3/?__vbtrk=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
---
Friend, we are on pace for a historic victory!
I am so thankful for all of the support I have already received from across the Empire State and the nation from patriots like you that are committed to saving America from Radical Leftists like Zohran Mamdani and Kathy Hochul.
Thanks to you this race in a traditionally Democrat stronghold is already within the margin of error.
**New York is now a toss-up state!**
Our message of delivering a safer and more affordable state for all is resonating with New Yorkers who are tired of nearly two decades of single party Democrat rule currently led by the embattled and deeply unpopular Governor Kathy Hochul.
**[Kathy Hochul is the _WORST_ governor in America.](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byE3/?__vbtrk=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**

[SAVE NEW YORK](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byE3/?__vbtrk=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
Under her abysmal leadership New York has implemented** _failed bail reform_** and was** _cemented as a sanctuary state_**. The results of these decisions have been devastating as violent criminals are routinely put back on our streets to recommit violent crimes and we are seeing a rise in crime from illegal immigrants.
Her record on handling the state’s economy is even worse. She has** _raised taxes_** and pushed failed policies that** _raised the cost of living_**. With Kathy Hochul at the helm New York has some of the highest taxes, rent, energy prices, utility bills, and cost of groceries in the country.
And when New Yorkers looked to Governor Hochul to rein in the extremism emerging from her own party she decided to bend the knee. She** _helped elect Zohran Mamdani_** , who is a communist and jihadist sympathizer, as Mayor of NYC.
To borrow a phrase from Governor Hochul herself:**"This bullsh*t has to stop"**.
It is clear that Hochul is out of touch and New Yorkers are ready for new leadership. But now it is up to us to lay the groundwork for victory next year.
Every major Democrat donor in the country is being called upon to save Kathy Hochul from her coming reckoning at the ballot box in a last-ditch effort to keep New York blue.
**Firing Hochul and flipping New York red is going to be a team effort** and I need every one of my patriotic supporters right now to help me deliver my message to every voter across the Empire State.

[FIRE KATHY HOCHUL](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byE3/?__vbtrk=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
Friend, here is how you can help today.
My team just dropped a new ad that I want every New Yorker to see:

[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byE3/?__vbtrk=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
_NEW AD: Kathy’s Own Words: This Bullsh*t has to stop._

[WATCH ELISE’S NEW AD](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byE3/?__vbtrk=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
To defeat Hochul we need to expose her failed record and provide voters with an alternative vision for the state. This ad helps with that mission.
**_[So please, take a minute to watch my new ad, send it to every New Yorker you know, and make a contribution of whatever you can so we can keep it running](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byE3/?__vbtrk=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_****.**
This is our opportunity to SAVE NEW YORK!
I am grateful for all of the support I am receiving to win this fight. Thank you for continuing to stand by my side.
**Let’s finish the fight to make New York affordable and safe again.**

_Your fellow patriot,_
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byE3/?__vbtrk=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
**Elise Stefanik**
Proud New Yorker
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/byE3/?__vbtrk=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzM2MTk3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

subject: Arrest Made in Biddeford and Falmouth Walmart Robberies
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** Arrest Made in Biddeford and Falmouth Walmart Robberies (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=7924a070a3&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Jon Fetherston on Nov 28, 2025 04:57 pm
A Portland man has been charged in connection with robberies at Walmart stores in Biddeford and Falmouth earlier this month, authorities said. Biddeford police said officers responded to a Nov. 9 robbery at the Biddeford Walmart, where a suspect fled the scene in a vehicle. Investigators later identified a potential suspect and vehicle through the [...]

A full-blown legal fight has broken out between Nova Scotia’s entrenched lobstermen and indigenous fishermen with close relatives in Maine. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

👉 [Click here to uncover the move hiding in plain sight.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=c77eae7c96524e64a0abc9d3dcbf953b&_e=LB_E9PtBxrmITL5HxBZHdN7Qq2FS1aOyJXMGdidQDaHCzAfFA_HTTvt5xONFIt35tr1dZP5iCzAM8oKkQHjX75740ahpMuMmm64pIw5b4zMsDdygtU4Kr5XbWIXMAQr2ylh4E6xOJKwQem5PuBqyj1_qi-g_hhOu8nJv2yAYpBaE_IQjfth2PcNKHnVTWTsnmUuaIQiIjkfg04Rhx0TRcCcG84CTjo4j5ueHVG0KgO7xs654fjpydwa50H4ENRdxOe9dgsISDEU4BnqtfRokPIAbPN1FquRzbikfcxC7iKZFFqH6fi53xplIaY-68qwZktfb7JLVApu_AofsHkfjyoLab-Es7itF5-pRbo4fw-ETHATgvI7bqzcj3-LneX_HBnG2rac16wzBcSg_rPlj74ZwTlxWAyISghOWZTs0PGS7u-yQFVtGsIqU-D0rQyTQ_mfrZZqskbaCs15ghyGYlL19An0fAuBwkdPGnYS23KuWNkLuCfL5bFwAqfoP5kt_Twcs6muwU4fSEzrgjun4YIk7KE0dyHSi0YOO46jyEWlCiPPkTexkpm-aCmBOdsEGWajVnR7ZqWdJ_Zyi7hRTWg%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. | | | Small-town dad taps his phone to pay for gas... But it’s not just a payment — it’s a clue to a hidden strategy. 👉 Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

_Behind the Markets_
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: BlackRock just funneled $225 billion into a hidden AI frontier, and Buffett quickly followed with $40 billion of his own.| | | | November 29 | | | | | AI’s Next Giant Is Still Underpriced Now | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. clean_body: Charlie showed us how we take back our culture — for good.| | | Fellow Patriot,Charlie Kirk knew something long before the rest of America caught on. That the next generation of young Americans were desperate for truth... That the Left's grip on the culture wasn't as strong as it looked... And with boldness, hard work, and a focused strategy -- WE COULD WIN. Charlie didn't just talk about it. He built a movement that's changed history. And long before he left us... he wrote How We Win -- the playbook that lays it all out. Today, Turning Point USA wants to send it to you for FREE when you make a gift of any amount to carry Charlie's mission forward. Fellow Patriot, this isn't just a book... It 's the exact blueprint Charlie used to: 🔥 Launch Turning Point USA chapters in all 50 states 🔥 Wake up more than 820,000 student activists 🔥 Inspire a historic generational shift toward FREEDOM Charlie saw it coming before anyone else did. He showed the world that the American Culture War wasn't lost. While the media said Gen Z was gone, Charlie believed in them. He met them face to face. On the campuses. At the rallies. In the fight. He proved the Left and the Fake News Media wrong. He showed us HOW WE WIN. Now it's up to us to finish what he started. You and I have the opportunity to carry his torch, reach the next student, change more hearts and minds, and save the future of America. But we can't do it without grassroots patriots like you. How We Win is the game plan -- written in Charlie's words, forged in real fights, and ready to be handed to the next bold American who says "YES, I'll stand up." Let that be you, Fellow Patriot. This book isn't a political piece... It's not about playing games in the D.C. swamp... It's a blueprint for taking back our culture and rebuilding the foundations of this country. Faith. Freedom. As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. name: Adam Schiff
subject: Trump publicly demanded I be indicted.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action
body:
| | | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4c5f5de0-c1ab-4bfe-b36f-2218aeef5a6d/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Peter — This is Adam Schiff. I wanted to bring you up to speed about one of Trump’s demands that I be punished for impeaching him and serving on the January 6th Committee. Trump publicly pressured his former personal attorney and now-Attorney General, Pam Bondi, to punish his political enemies, including me. He said that I was “guilty as hell,” that the Department of Justice “can’t delay any longer,” and that “JUSTICE MUST BE SERVED, NOW!!!” [I need you to stand with me right now. Trump is ramping up his public threats to abuse the justice system to punish me for my work to hold him accountable. Split a donation between my campaigns and Blue Amp Action today:](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta0c07ed3-c6dc-46a9-be23-cf8f194f5d79/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

First, Donald Trump fired a U.S. Attorney in Virginia. And Trump was explicit about why he was firing him. It was because this U.S. Attorney resisted efforts by Trump and his Department of Justice to charge New York Attorney General Letitia James with the crimes that Trump already said she was “guilty” of, but for which there was no evidence. The implication is clear: if prosecutors don’t go after me and the president’s other enemies with meritless cases, they too will be fired. This is the biggest attack on the rule of law and independent justice since Watergate. After Nixon’s abuses of the Justice Department during Watergate, a wall was erected between the White House and Department of Justice to try and prevent a president from surreptitiously ordering the prosecution of his enemies. Now the wall is obliterated — and all of the corruption and weaponization is out in the open. [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4bb6ed5c-33a8-4c44-a548-657473c71ee3/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade, it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in a democracy, not a dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T07b9c82a-33e9-41c9-93ca-907f79898505/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Haley Stevens
subject: I wanted to fight for the communities I love
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
We have the power to chart a new path

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1503%2E49239%2EswVeuy)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9545?t=2&akid=1503%2E49239%2EswVeuy)
---
[](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9546?t=3&akid=1503%2E49239%2EswVeuy)
**I bet you still remember getting handed the keys to your first car, null.** I sure do.
That old, used Cutlass Supreme? It meant more to me than just freedom.
I always knew I wanted to spend my life fighting for the people and communities I love. When the Great Recession hit and the American car industry faced extinction, I wanted to step up and do something for my state. So I answered the call to serve as chief of staff to President Obama’s auto rescue initiative, and we helped save 200,000 Michigan jobs and stabilize our economy.
Fast forward to today, and Michigan jobs are once again at risk, this time from Donald Trump and his reckless tariffs – which are threatening the jobs of tens of thousands of hardworking Michiganders.
**Trump said he “couldn’t care less” if auto prices rise.**
Well, as someone who has spent my time in Congress fighting for Michigan jobs, Michigan families, and Michigan workers, _I couldn’t care more_.
That’s why I’m in this race, null – because just like that day I got the keys to my old Cutlass, we have the power to chart a new path. I’m determined to become Michigan’s next senator, stop Trump’s chaos, and keep fighting for working families here in Michigan and across the country. But I need your help.
Our race in Michigan will decide Senate control, and _The Cook Political Report_ rated it a “Toss Up.” The GOP is going to come after me with everything they’ve got, and I can’t fight back without your grassroots support. That’s why it’s so important to reach our fundraising goals – and why I’m personally asking:
**[Will you chip in $5 – or whatever you can give today – to help me hit tomorrow's $350,000 end-of-month goal so I can win in Michigan, take back the Senate for Democrats, and keep up my fight for working families?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9546?t=4&akid=1503%2E49239%2EswVeuy)**
_Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue._ | | | [Donate $5](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9547?t=5&akid=1503%2E49239%2EswVeuy)
---
| | [Donate $25](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9548?t=6&akid=1503%2E49239%2EswVeuy)
---
| | [Donate $50](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9549?t=7&akid=1503%2E49239%2EswVeuy)
---
| | [Donate Another Amount](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9546?t=8&akid=1503%2E49239%2EswVeuy)
---
Thank you so much for your early support. It will make such a huge difference.

Haley Stevens
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** Null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9550?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=9&akid=1503%2E49239%2EswVeuy) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/9545?t=10&akid=1503%2E49239%2EswVeuy)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley
