clean_body: Here are members of our campaign team helping the Metropolitan Detroit Diaper Bank package up pajamas and school supplies for kids in Metro Detroit: | | | Friend, From rising costs to cuts in nutrition and healthcare services, our communities are hurting. We created the Rooted in Community Leadership PAC to invest in our communities and ensure no one goes without. Each month, our campaign team is pitching in at food pantries, community dinners, and more. Here we are this month helping fill backpacks with school supplies and pajamas for local school kids: We’re also providing direct financial support, donating $1,000 each to 9 food banks during the government shutdown earlier this month, and donating nearly $100,000 this year, mostly to nonprofit organizations in our communities. | Thank you for always putting community first. -Rashida \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Rashida Tlaib** Date: Fri, Nov 28, 2025 Subject: From diaper banks to food pantries, we’re making sure no one goes without this winter To: dpwillis67@gmail.com Friend, This Black Friday, instead of bargain shopping on Amazon, I hope you’ll consider supporting families in need. When we created our Rooted in Community Leadership PAC (RIC PAC), it was always about much more than training young people and candidates to run for office and give back to their communities. It was also about direct community support. This year alone, RIC PAC has donated nearly $100,000, mostly to nonprofits providing critical services to our communities. Members of our campaign are in the community every week, whether it’s supporting diaper drives, collecting canned food, or helping out at community dinners. Here are members of our campaign team helping the Metropolitan Detroit Diaper Bank package up pajamas and school supplies for kids in Metro Detroit: We supported the Elmwood Blessing Box this year with direct financial support, allowing them to purchase diapers and period supplies. And, we’re adopting families this Christmas through local nonprofits, including Goodfellows. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | Your support makes all of this possible. Thank you for your generosity this holiday season. In love and solidarity, Rashida * | Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003, not authorized by any candidate or candidates committee. | | Sent via . name: Wes Moore
subject: We’re in a moment of danger and uncertainty
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
But true leaders don’t pick moments. Moments pick leaders — and the ones who step up are the ones we remember.
Hey Peter, it’s Governor Wes Moore. Heartland Patriots said I could reach out with an update.
Sunday, November 30th is the latest major fundraising deadline since we launched our reelection campaign.
**[That’s why I’m asking for your support before Sunday. Can you chip in a split donation between our campaign and Heartland Patriots today?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98348?refcode=em20251129-9773&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9773%2E614464%2Erkf2wp)**
We are in a moment of danger and uncertainty right now. But true leaders don’t pick moments. **Moments pick leaders — and the ones who step up are the ones we remember.**
We need to double down now, when times seem toughest, so we can protect our hard-fought progress.
Folks like you power everything we do. If you’ve been waiting for a time to step up and make a donation, this week’s deadline is a major one.
Here’s why: Three years ago, we flipped Maryland from red to blue. Now, Republicans are keeping a close eye on our fundraising to see if they can defeat me and take back Government House in 2026.
They have multiple potential candidates against me and they’re testing which one will be strongest. We have to prove that enough people are on board with our mission to beat anything they throw our way.
If we fall short, Republicans will seize on it as proof they can outspend us, and roll back the progress we’ve made together.
**[So what do you say, Peter? Can you make a split contribution of $10 or any amount between Heartland Patriots and me before Sunday's deadline?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98348?refcode=em20251129-9773&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9773%2E614464%2Erkf2wp)**
If you have stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Wes Moore and Heartland Patriots will process instantly:
[Contribute $10](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98349?refcode=em20251129-9773&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=3&akid=9773%2E614464%2Erkf2wp) [Contribute $25](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98349?refcode=em20251129-9773&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=25&t=4&akid=9773%2E614464%2Erkf2wp) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98349?refcode=em20251129-9773&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=5&akid=9773%2E614464%2Erkf2wp) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98349?refcode=em20251129-9773&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=6&akid=9773%2E614464%2Erkf2wp) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98349?refcode=em20251129-9773&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=7&akid=9773%2E614464%2Erkf2wp) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98349?refcode=em20251129-9773&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=8&akid=9773%2E614464%2Erkf2wp) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98348?refcode=em20251129-9773&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=9&akid=9773%2E614464%2Erkf2wp)

Peter, there’s been a lot going on in the battle for the House majority. Here’s a quick recap of the last few weeks for you: **_First,_** Republicans refused to negotiate while Americans struggled to make ends meet and healthcare costs skyrocketed, resulting in the longest government shutdown in our nation’s history. **_Then,_** earlier this month, voters all across the country delivered a strong rebuke of Donald Trump and sent a resounding message: they are fed up with chaos, uncertainty, and the same failed leadership. **_Now,_** as we head toward 2026, we have the opportunity to keep that momentum going, and it starts with showing the strength of our grassroots team. Every contribution, no matter the size, fuels the work that will ensure North Carolina’s 4th District has a representative who truly fights for them. Republicans are more beholden to Trump’s extreme agenda and their billionaire donors than they are to the communities they serve. **[Valerie stands for something different. Every day, she shows up focused on delivering real results for the people of the 4th District. subject: ✨We’re Building Something Big for 2026 — And Today’s Your Moment to Join Us
body:
Blue Friday was huge. Now we need everyone who's been thinking about joining to step in. 75% off.

| |
---|---|---
|
# ✨We're Building Something Big for 2026 -- And Today's Your Moment to Join Us
### Blue Friday was huge. Now we need everyone who's been thinking about joining to step in. 75% off.
| | | [Cliff Schecter](https://substack.com/@cliffsedge) and [Blue Amp](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---
| Nov 29
---
| [](https://substack.com/@cliffsedge)| | [](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/052b310f-4179-4af5-9d23-4f15c2329e4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Hey friends,
Cliff here.
I want to take a minute to thank everyone who joined us yesterday on **Blue Friday**. It meant the world -- and it showed that there's a real hunger out there for independent voices that don't bow, don't scrape, and don't pretend fascism is just another "viewpoint."
Yesterday wasn't about buying cheap stuff.
It was about building power.
And now we keep going.
Because the truth is this:
🟦 **[We're building something big for 2026.](https://substack.com/redirect/052b310f-4179-4af5-9d23-4f15c2329e4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
* Bigger investigations.
* Bigger reporting.
* More video.
* More voices.
* More reach.
* More truth.
* More fight.

[$19.99 Helps Us Build For 2026!](https://substack.com/redirect/052b310f-4179-4af5-9d23-4f15c2329e4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
We want to turn the entire damn map solid blue -- and we need you in the coalition that's going to do it.
If you were thinking about joining but got busy yesterday, or you opened the email and thought, _" I'll come back to this,"_ this is your moment.
🔥 **[75% off an annual BAM membership-- just $19.99.](https://substack.com/redirect/052b310f-4179-4af5-9d23-4f15c2329e4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[
](https://substack.com/redirect/052b310f-4179-4af5-9d23-4f15c2329e4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)🔥 **[Our best offer ever.](https://substack.com/redirect/052b310f-4179-4af5-9d23-4f15c2329e4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
This isn't Black Friday consumerism.
This is building a pro-democracy movement that corporate media can't buy and billionaires can't muzzle.
You've been here with us.
You've read us.
You've supported us in spirit.
And now we're asking you to step in for real.
👉 **[Join us for $19.99.](https://substack.com/redirect/052b310f-4179-4af5-9d23-4f15c2329e4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[
](https://substack.com/redirect/052b310f-4179-4af5-9d23-4f15c2329e4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Help us scale up for the most important election year of our lives.
We're rising.
We're growing.
And with your help, we're about to make a hell of a lot of noise.
See you inside,
**Cliff**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/052b310f-4179-4af5-9d23-4f15c2329e4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

subject: How we won in 2025
body:

body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Our movement is built on one simple idea: people, not billionaires, hold
the power. This month has shown just how much we can accomplish when we
fight together — but the work isn’t done.

Our end-of-month fundraising deadline is just around the corner. Every
goal we set isn’t just a number. It’s a step toward building a bloc of
working-class leaders in Congress who will fight for the people’s agenda,
not corporate interests. Every goal we hit means more power for
communities, more voices in Congress, and more bold action on the issues
that actually matter — healthcare, climate justice, workers’ rights, and
racial and economic equity.

Here’s where you stand:

Supporter Record for
Peter

yet!

victories that can change lives.

---|---|---
The Thanksgiving holiday in the United States was marked by darkness and tragedy amid the aftermath of the terrorist attack on two West Virginia National Guardsmen who were on patrol in the nation's capital.
Here is what we know so far: The suspected shooter is Rahmanullah Lakanwal. He is originally from Afghanistan and he entered the United States in 2021 as part of a U.S. government program following the Biden-Harris administration's disastrous withdrawal from Afghanistan.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..LBgR6TGaH2RbxyN1sjT1o9SDFA3XM9T2ZSNbGGX_Two?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=180254547)
Lakanwal was supposedly "vetted" because he had worked for a CIA unit. He eventually settled in Washington state and traveled to Washington, D.C. to carry out his ambush.
I was told by a trusted law enforcement source Lakanwal had shouted "Allahu ackbar!" while he shot the National Guardsmen with a revolver and then one of their own firearms. A National Guard supervisor, who was checking on the lower enlisted and had just left the two service members, ran behind cover. As the suspect was reloading, the Guardsman used a pocket knife to stab him. A different Guardsman ran to the scene and shot the suspect multiple times.
* * *
# **[Say NO to Communism!](https://substack.com/redirect/46a797df-c462-449f-b104-50f148c0028d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
## **Get your "Communism Kills" Commie Mamdani shirt here:**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/46a797df-c462-449f-b104-50f148c0028d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Shop Now](https://substack.com/redirect/46a797df-c462-449f-b104-50f148c0028d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
This highlights that the Biden-Harris administration's ill-advised efforts to juice their "rescue" numbers at the expense of national security will be with us for the time being. **What makes Lakanwal 's case worse is that he was apparently vetted, while there were thousands of people who were not properly vetted** during the chaotic evacuation. Then again, any service member who deployed to Afghanistan will tell you not only did they have to worry about the Taliban and al-Qaeda, but they had to be on guard against insider attacks from supposed Afghan allies.
These "green on blue" attacks were far from rare during the United States' 20-year long war in that country.
Democrats have naturally and disgustingly blamed President Donald Trump for the attack, saying the National Guard should not be in D.C. to begin with. Putting aside their connotation that the Guardsmen deserved it for deploying to D.C., their presence and security posture has resulted in a lower crime rate when compared to the same time last year. Fewer murders, fewer robberies, etc. Non-TDS riddled D.C. citizens have noticed the improvement in public safety since the Guardsmen have been in the city.
Democrats have likewise said Trump bears the blame because Lakanwal reportedly got his asylum case approved in early 2025. What they are not realizing is they are making the case for mass deportations.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..LBgR6TGaH2RbxyN1sjT1o9SDFA3XM9T2ZSNbGGX_Two?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=180254547)
Even though the suspect was properly "vetted," was a trusted ally to work with the CIA, and had his asylum case approved means we simply can not trust the government's process to keep Americans safe. Americans
The Trump administration had already taken steps to look through the people that were brought over from Afghanistan under Biden, but now those efforts are being redoubled.
At the time of this writing, it was just announced National Guardsman Sarah Beckstrom had succumbed to her injuries.
Americans and the safety of its citizens must always come first.
* * *

body:

| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tfc729d16-3041-497f-b8a5-5b535fcbaf18/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

| Peter, As you read this, Jon Ossoff is getting absolutely pummeled by attack ads in Georgia. Republicans have spent more than $10 million on TV and online already. It’s ridiculous. Why so much money, this far from Election Day? Because Jon Ossoff is the only Democratic Senator running for reelection in a state won by Trump. That is why Republicans are hitting him so hard, so early in the campaign. _And it’s why we’re coming to you with this request to help fight back._ If Jon is going to defend this seat, we really need to keep hitting our fundraising goals, and donations to emails like this are a huge part of how it happens. [Can Jon Ossoff count on you to contribute $5, $50, or whatever you can afford to help him lay the foundation for a winning Senate reelection campaign here in Georgia? Your donation will be split between his reelection campaign and Blue Amp Action.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T46c00bc3-c52a-4110-bef3-63f473512a82/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| We wish we didn’t have to ask at all, but Citizens United opened the dam for limitless dark money to flood into our elections. That’s a big part of why Congress is broken and corrupted. It’s why we have really expensive elections. And also, it’s why campaigns like ours have to build serious grassroots fundraising operations from the very start. Because if we don’t, we run the risk of our message getting drowned out by a flood of Super PAC spending. So here’s the truth: Jon needs our help today, Peter. He is literally MAGA’s #1 target. They will spare no expense and try every dirty trick in the book. So yes, this election will be tough. But this team knows how to win tough fights. We’ve done it before. Let’s do it again. [Please, use this link to support Jon’s reelection campaign and Blue Amp Action. Every donation counts. Every donation sends a powerful message to MAGA that we’re ready to fight and win.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0fa793e7-ef08-4b8f-9390-3532dc27b6d5/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade, it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in a democracy, not a dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9380830f-b6f4-45ec-b86c-adec45707af2/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

subject: This has gone on long enough

body:
After our royal shellacking on November 4th, I sent out an email with a survey attached, but only a few opened it, much less filled it out.
After our royal shellacking on November 4th, I sent out an email with a survey attached, but only a few opened it, much less filled it out.

| | Fellow American, One takeaway that I've been consistently seeing in the aftermath of the election losses on November 4th is that the media - left, right, and center- think we (The GOP) are rudderless; that we don't stand for anything and don't have a plan to govern. I sent you a survey immediately after our election night losses to help me better understand how we can retain your vote and support in Congress, but I didn't hear back. **_I 'm resending the survey because it's of extreme importance not only to me, but also to the future success of our conservative movement. _** I really need you to fill it out without skipping over any questions. _**_Can you do this as a personal favor to me?

With Steve’s leadership, Minnesota has led the nation in voter turnout for the past three out of four elections. His top priorities are defending our democracy, strengthening the freedom to vote for all eligible Minnesotans, pushing back against dangerous disinformation about our elections, providing support for small businesses, and enhancing security protections for our most vulnerable. name: Roy Cooper
subject: Sorry to interrupt your Saturday morning…
body:
I just had a check-in with my team.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1898?t=1&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)
Hi Peter — I’m sorry to email so early during your holiday weekend, but with only hours remaining in the month, my team and I are having frequent check-ins on the progress to our November fundraising goal. I just heard from them again, and I had to reach out right away: We’re still not at our $825,000 online target, and we’re running out of time to reach it. **[Before I explain more, can I ask you to pitch to help close the gap?](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2083?t=6&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)** | **[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2083?t=7&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)**
---
Peter, I wish I didn’t have to ask for your support so often, but we will not succeed without your help.
Republicans are already investing heavily against me, and we expect them to spend hundreds of millions more in the months to come — this could potentially become the first U.S. Senate race ever to cost a billion dollars. They’re lining up PACs, dark-money groups, and mega-donors to write endless checks to benefit my opponent. Talk about absurd.
We need to be able to take them on — dollar for dollar. That’s why missing this month’s fundraising goal could set us back significantly, but I know that our team will step up before time runs out.
**[I’m personally asking for your support right now. Please give anything that fits in your budget.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2083?t=8&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
[DONATE $5](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2084?t=9&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)
---
[DONATE $10](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2085?t=10&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)
---
[DONATE $25](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2086?t=11&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)
---
[DONATE $50](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2087?t=12&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)
---
[DONATE $100](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2088?t=13&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)
---
[DONATE ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2089?t=14&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)
---
I deeply appreciate all your help,
Roy Cooper
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2083?t=15&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?t=2&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)**
[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?t=3&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)
---
[DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1915?t=4&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=5&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

[You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time.](http://act.roycooper.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)
If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, [submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=16&akid=72%2E124072%2E-yOnsv)

body:
Mark Kelly’s most important supporters aren’t the people who write the biggest checks.
#

Friend —
Mark Kelly’s most important supporters aren’t the people who write the biggest checks.
They’re the people who were there when it mattered most.
People like you.
---

Your support has been critical in helping Mark combat MAGA extremism.
You helped us flip this seat in Arizona in 2020 and re-elect Mark in 2022. And now, it’s helping Mark fight back against his most serious challenge yet — threats of arrest and execution from the President of the United States. Seriously.

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: Your Giving Tuesday gift will restore the First Amendment
body:
The countdown to Giving Tuesday is on, and your support matters.
[](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation/1/0100019ad081507f-0bb157a8-3064-444c-9224-60828bba3f2c-000000/8YBvOLn0Eg7bROYtlXnyd4hfH4ck7c9gusd1-gPRbq0=433)Patriot–I hope you had a meaningful and restful Thanksgiving with the people you love. As we turn from gratitude to giving, I want to share how [_**your support this** **Giving Tuesday** **will make a real and lasting impact**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation%2Fyearend%2F/1/0100019ad081507f-0bb157a8-3064-444c-9224-60828bba3f2c-000000/NU088eqqy78eHbn1QWdPBOPMO8RiF7UzJli5bbQdGCU=433) on our country.As you know, next year, America turns 250 years old — a once-in-a-lifetime milestone. And **with your help, Tea Party Patriots Foundation will mark that anniversary with the America250 Free Speech Revival Bus Tour,** a cross-country effort to educate, equip, and inspire citizens to use their First Amendment rights of free speech, peaceful assembly, and petitioning the government.From April through June of 2026, we will travel to 45 cities in more than two dozen states, calling Americans to renewed courage and civic action. [_**Your Giving Tuesday gift will make these gatherings possible**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation%2Fyearend%2F/2/0100019ad081507f-0bb157a8-3064-444c-9224-60828bba3f2c-000000/Q6hMi0UlCQDhflEIAZyMT33CL31ooEcHU526H4RC4tI=433). At these events, citizens will learn deeply about what the First Amendment protects, how to defend it, and how to use it. They will also gain practical skills for organizing in their communities and speaking out with clarity and conviction.Our program will focus on adults because, while educating children is essential, it is parents and grandparents who carry the responsibility of passing on America’s values and history. If they are not grounded in our founding principles, the next generation will be unprepared to keep our republic.**Giving Tuesday is your opportunity to help ensure America’s 250th birthday is not merely a ceremony, but a** **true revival of freedom** — with the First Amendment restored to its rightful place at the heart of our civic life.[_**Your tax-deductible gift today or on Giving Tuesday**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation%2Fyearend%2F/3/0100019ad081507f-0bb157a8-3064-444c-9224-60828bba3f2c-000000/6eQrf-OP2a5ghRuysEC09bd3cgiLPeJMg2rDCoIAZtk=433)**will help reinvigorate our First Amendment and restore the spirit of liberty for America’s next 250 years and beyond.**[ SUPPORT THE FIRST AMENDMENT](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation%2Fyearend%2F/4/0100019ad081507f-0bb157a8-3064-444c-9224-60828bba3f2c-000000/UfIn9kWS2jhX2H6VJFVANlsIV2t_eqZjEPr5_BMvyRk=433)As we prepare for this historic journey, your partnership means more than you know. Every gift — large or small — fuels a movement to restore America’s First Amendment freedoms and strengthen the next generation’s understanding of our founding ideals. **Thank you for giving, for believing in this work, and for helping safeguard America’s future.** In liberty,Jenny Beth[CONTRIBUTE](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation%2Fyearend%2F/5/0100019ad081507f-0bb157a8-3064-444c-9224-60828bba3f2c-000000/lMzbzMzVxg1QV_2kGnBzNABjuDlhg6J3FAdtBJFRrOc=433) _This email list is a joint venture of the following separate organizations: Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc., Tea Party Patriots Foundation, and Tea Party Patriots Citizens Fund. All rights reserved.__Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc. operates as a social welfare organization under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code. Contributions to Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc. are not deductible as charitable contributions for income tax purposes._ To donate by mail, send your check made payable to:[_**Tea Party Patriots Foundation**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Ffoundation%2Fdonate%2F/1/0100019ad081507f-0bb157a8-3064-444c-9224-60828bba3f2c-000000/8AhCaokZcyIuDVQniAnr-CkUeLKEkO-B6atvgkfZgJk=433)
2295 Towne Lake Pkwy Ste 116-314
Woodstock, GA 30189Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [unsubscribe](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftpot.teapartypatriots.org%2Fcontact-preferences%2F01jp3rk1m05jb3jy5xyw9vqr5t%3Fct=1ebd6a5833cd%26et=a8fe34ad85d9/1/0100019ad081507f-0bb157a8-3064-444c-9224-60828bba3f2c-000000/E8gWcJNqojyTLSPgqUKwZlPh_yhy8doYDduShEi6w5k=433) or [update your preferences](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftpot.teapartypatriots.org%2Fcontact-preferences%2F01jp3rk1m05jb3jy5xyw9vqr5t%3Fct=1ebd6a5833cd%26et=a8fe34ad85d9/2/0100019ad081507f-0bb157a8-3064-444c-9224-60828bba3f2c-000000/qSmKIGypvWtU9JoBOjR71rr6MgTjIH12xBbFTG4IZyA=433).

name: PramilaForCongress.com
subject: Just one day left
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
We need all hands on deck to hit our end-of-month goal.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20251129-5657-footer&t=1&akid=5657%2E420207%2EbtUEoU)
Hi Peter, Team Pramila here with a quick update:
**Our end-of-month deadline hits at midnight tomorrow night — and we’re still $5,000 short of hitting our $33,000 goal.**
There’s too much at stake and we've come too far to fail now. So we’ve been checking our supporter records to see who might step up to help power this final push. Here’s what we have for you:
**Pramila For Congress** Record for: Peter November Donor: Not yet Deadline: Sunday Night Suggested Action: **[Contribute $3](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251129-1.q_j6yi&amount=3&refcode=em20251129-5657&t=2&akid=5657%2E420207%2EbtUEoU)**
---
**This isn’t just any deadline, Peter.** On Monday, Pramila will return to Congress to lead the fight to stop Republicans from letting health care subsidies expire — a move that would rip coverage from millions. While she’s focused on that fight, she’s counting on us to reach our campaign fundraising goals.
And it’s not just health care — she’s fighting to hold ICE accountable and organize nationwide to resist Trump’s authoritarianism and flip the House in 2026. That can only happen with [**grassroots supporters like you stepping up to fuel her work and keep this movement strong.**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251129-1.q_j6yi&refcode=em20251129-5657&amount=3&t=3&akid=5657%2E420207%2EbtUEoU)
This is also our first major test since voters rejected Trumpism and progressives won big earlier this month. MAGA is betting we’ll ease up and treat those wins like the finish line — not the beginning. Let’s show them we’re just getting started.
**If you haven’t had a chance to give this month, this is the time that your support matters most.** Every single dollar raised before tomorrow night will keep Pramila focused on fighting for us, not dialing for dollars.
[**If you’re able to, please make your first donation this month to help us close the gap before tomorrow’s midnight deadline and power this movement into 2026. We can’t do it without you!**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251129-1.q_j6yi&refcode=em20251129-5657&amount=3&t=4&akid=5657%2E420207%2EbtUEoU)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20251129-1.q_j6yi&amount=3&refcode=em20251129-5657&t=23&akid=5657%2E420207%2EbtUEoU)**
In solidarity,
Pramila for Congress

clean_body: We need all hands on deck to hit our end-of-month goal. Hi Peter, Team Pramila here with a quick update: Our end-of-month deadline hits at midnight tomorrow night — and we’re still $5,000 short of hitting our $33,000 goal. There’s too much at stake and we've come too far to fail now. So we’ve been checking our supporter records to see who might step up to help power this final push. Here’s what we have for you: Pramila For Congress Record for: Peter November Donor: Not yet Deadline: Sunday Night Suggested Action: This isn’t just any deadline, Peter. On Monday, Pramila will return to Congress to lead the fight to stop Republicans from letting health care subsidies expire — a move that would rip coverage from millions. While she’s focused on that fight, she’s counting on us to reach our campaign fundraising goals. And it’s not just health care — she’s fighting to hold ICE accountable and organize nationwide to resist Trump’s authoritarianism and flip the House in 2026. That can only happen with This is also our first major test since voters rejected Trumpism and progressives won big earlier this month. MAGA is betting we’ll ease up and treat those wins like the finish line — not the beginning. Let’s show them we’re just getting started. If you haven’t had a chance to give this month, this is the time that your support matters most. Every single dollar raised before tomorrow night will keep Pramila focused on fighting for us, not dialing for dollars. In solidarity, Pramila for Congress | | Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com

name: Team DPVA
subject: Before November ends, here’s what’s at stake in Virginia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic Party of Virginia, www
body:
We have the mandate, now we have to make it count.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120224896/585619254/-793144256?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkOTUzYWZkZi00NGNkLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WqH9XY96uQpmfwPcVm6pHiExfABGVFeld5S8TjrjD8Y=&emci=fe665d4c-2bca-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=d953afdf-44cd-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=31008101)
---
Hi Peter, This month, voters elected Governor Abigail Spanberger, Lieutenant Governor Ghazala Hashmi, and Attorney General Jay Jones, and delivered one of the strongest Democratic majorities our Commonwealth has seen in a generation. That victory wasn’t just a win. It was a mandate. Now comes the part that matters most: governing well, protecting Virginians, and delivering on the progress people voted for. And the truth is, the work doesn’t get easier after an election; it gets more urgent. Here’s what’s ahead for Virginia in the months to come: **1\. The General Assembly convenes in January, and Democrats will be tasked with:**
* Protecting reproductive freedom
* Strengthening public education
* Lowering costs for working families
* Tackling corporate price gouging
* Expanding access to health care and childcare
* Advancing gun safety
* Rebuilding infrastructure and climate resilience

Progress doesn’t happen automatically. It requires strategy, organizing, and a strong statewide party behind our lawmakers. **2\. Trump’s 2025 agenda will directly impact Virginia families**
Even after our victory, national policies like tariffs, federal rollbacks, and Project 2025 continue to threaten household budgets, worker protections, civil rights, and federal support for our economy and our military communities. We must continue to be the firewall, and that requires ongoing investment, not one election night. **3\. The 2026 midterms start now**
Republicans are already recruiting candidates and targeting the very districts we just flipped. If we wait until next year to rebuild the field, we’ll be starting behind. [**That’s why your end-of-month support matters so much. Your contribution today helps DPVA keep organizers in the field year-round, strengthen local DECs and committees across the Commonwealth, train new volunteers and precinct leaders, and build the statewide infrastructure Democrats will need heading into 2026. If you can, chip in $10 before midnight to help us power the next chapter of this work.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120224897/585619255/688982926?&refcode=E16387-20251129-EOM3-SL-DeadlineEOM&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkOTUzYWZkZi00NGNkLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WqH9XY96uQpmfwPcVm6pHiExfABGVFeld5S8TjrjD8Y=) | **************[Chip in to keep Virginia moving forward →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120224898/585619256/688982926?&refcode=E16387-20251129-EOM3-SB-DeadlineEOM&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkOTUzYWZkZi00NGNkLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WqH9XY96uQpmfwPcVm6pHiExfABGVFeld5S8TjrjD8Y=)**************

Thank you for being part of a movement that doesn’t stop at Election Day. The work continues, and because of you, so does our progress.
— Team DPVA

| | **A NOTE FROM CHRISSY**
---
| | **THREATS FOR DEFENDING THE CONSTITUTION**
---
The oath I took is to the Constitution. After releasing a video reminding military members they don't have to follow illegal orders, the President called me a traitor and suggested I be executed.

Now, the FBI has contacted the House Sergeant at Arms, requesting interviews with me and my colleagues.

This is an extraordinary and dangerous escalation. President Trump is using the FBI as a tool to intimidate and harass Members of Congress. We will not be bullied.

**[If you believe in defending our Constitution and standing up to the weaponization of federal power against political opponents, please stand with me today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120225100/585619534/685235109?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1191-ot-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMTUzYWZkZi00NGNkLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JtTPZkwljQRRmOUtjbd-hMtYyIN3tQ0cnB-7mzl2Law=)**
---
| | **Speaker johnson confrontation**
---
Earlier this month, I attended Speaker Johnson's press conference and asked him why he hadn't brought both parties together to end the shutdown – you may have seen the videos.

This was the longest government shutdown in U.S. history, at 43 days. During this time, I spent every day working with my Democratic colleagues in Washington to protect health care for Americans. _Republicans cannot say the same._

They refused to meet with us, which is why I had to bring our concerns to Speaker Johnson right on the Capitol steps, urging him to bring both parties' leaders together.

I will always show up for our communities, especially when this administration continues to harm our families, friends, and neighbors.
---
| | **Thank you, nancy pelosi**
---
Earlier this month, former Speaker Nancy Pelosi announced her retirement from Congress. The first woman to serve as Speaker of the House, she has spent her entire career fighting for the American people and has led House Democrats through some of the most important battles, including passing the Affordable Care Act and holding Donald Trump accountable.

Thank you, Madam Speaker, for an extraordinary 38 years of service. We wish you the best in this next chapter.
---
| | **2025 ELECTION**
---
Across the nation, we watched as Democrats delivered historic victories up and down the ballot – and Pennsylvania was no exception. We retained all three state Supreme Court justices, elected the first Democratic district attorney in Bucks County, and elected a Democratic county executive in Erie County.

Congratulations to all on these incredible victories. I cannot wait to see the great things they'll do for hardworking Pennsylvanians.
---
Thank you for your support, Derek.

There's work to be done,

Chrissy

**Donald Trump is ramping up his abuse of power:**

❌ Weaponzing the DOJ to BLOCK California’s new maps.

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: This official Apollo recording is only seconds long, but it will chill you to the bone.| | | | November 29 | | | | | What Neil Armstrong REALLY saw on the moon [TERRIFYING] | - | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: NDRC
subject: An existential threat to democracy in front of the Supreme Court
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
Rush a donation to help defend voting rights
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120225431/585620534/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImJmNTNhZmRmLTQ0Y2QtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=i-ggE-mjB55JmcNq3EsTVuKm4AWuiGEs3oQRdKxO-KQ=&emci=640528f8-e7ca-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=bf53afdf-44cd-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter, An attack on the crown jewel of the Civil Rights Movement is unfolding right now before the highest court in the land. This headline says it all: **“The Supreme Court is hearing a case that could weaken the Voting Rights Act — and upend the midterms” — _Politico_** The case, _Louisiana v. Callais_ , can be traced back to the state’s 2022 congressional map, which denied Black voters equal opportunity to elect a candidate of their choice. African Americans made up one-third of the state of Louisiana, but the map included just one Black-opportunity district out of the state’s six. The map was challenged in court, for diluting the voting power of Black Louisianans, prompting the state to redraw its map to include two Black-opportunity districts.

On October 15, the Supreme Court heard oral arguments about whether the redrawn, fairer map violates the 14th and 15th Amendments — **a question that could deem the Voting Rights Act itself unconstitutional.** It’s clear: We must be ready to respond when the Supreme Court decides the fate of Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act. And we have to be equipped with the resources to push back against the extreme attacks that will happen if the Supreme Court weakens this monumental protection — opening the floodgates for more redistricting chaos. **If the idea of the Supreme Court gutting the Voting Rights Act and ripping fair representation away from millions of voters enrages you, here’s what you can do about it:** [**Rush a donation to the NDRC and help make sure the fair maps movement has the resources we need to keep fighting back against extreme, anti-democracy efforts to weaken our voting rights.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120225434/585620536/-1263916904?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_CallaisHeadline_email_20251129-NDRC-Tier1-CallaisHeadline_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251129-NDRC-Tier1-CallaisHeadline_&utm_campaign=CallaisHeadline&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImJmNTNhZmRmLTQ0Y2QtZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=i-ggE-mjB55JmcNq3EsTVuKm4AWuiGEs3oQRdKxO-KQ=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: Rush a donation to help defend voting rights | Peter, An attack on the crown jewel of the Civil Rights Movement is unfolding right now before the highest court in the land. This headline says it all: “The Supreme Court is hearing a case that could weaken the Voting Rights Act — and upend the midterms” — Politico The case, Louisiana v. Callais , can be traced back to the state’s 2022 congressional map, which denied Black voters equal opportunity to elect a candidate of their choice. African Americans made up one-third of the state of Louisiana, but the map included just one Black-opportunity district out of the state’s six. The map was challenged in court, for diluting the voting power of Black Louisianans, prompting the state to redraw its map to include two Black-opportunity districts. On October 15, the Supreme Court heard oral arguments about whether the redrawn, fairer map violates the 14th and 15th Amendments — a question that could deem the Voting Rights Act itself unconstitutional. It’s clear: We must be ready to respond when the Supreme Court decides the fate of Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act. And we have to be equipped with the resources to push back against the extreme attacks that will happen if the Supreme Court weakens this monumental protection — opening the floodgates for more redistricting chaos. If the idea of the Supreme Court gutting the Voting Rights Act and ripping fair representation away from millions of voters enrages you, here’s what you can do about it: = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Thanks for your support, The NDRC Team = = | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC. Text MAPS to 36787, STOP to end. Msg and Data rates may apply

name: Leaders We Deserve
subject: Will you help Christian win his runoff?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Your support will help elect a proven fighter for his community and invest in long-term progressive power.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20251129-1&t=1&akid=2886%2E587540%2EgUMyNI)
Hi Peter. The countdown is on in Houston:
**Christian Menefee’s runoff is officially scheduled for January 31st** — a narrow window to finish what we started and elect a young progressive who will champion his community and fight corporate power in Washington.
But the timing couldn’t be tougher: **our end-of-month deadline hits TOMORROW at midnight** , and holiday giving dropped off right when we needed to surge. We’re inches away from winning Christian’s runoff, electing Akbar Ali in Georgia, and ramping up for 2026 — we _cannot_ afford to lose momentum now.
[**Will you split a contribution between Christian Menefee and Leaders We Deserve right now so we can hit our deadline and win this runoff?**](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251129_1_menefee.aSZvYS&refcode=em20251129-2886&amount=5&t=2&akid=2886%2E587540%2EgUMyNI)
**[Donate »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251129_1_menefee.aSZvYS&refcode=em20251129-2886&t=3&akid=2886%2E587540%2EgUMyNI)**
Christian has never backed down. As Harris County Attorney, he’s taken on major corporations and President Trump for targeting his community — and he’s beaten them again and again. Now he’s ready to take that fight to Congress for universal healthcare, affordability, and everyday families.
But he can’t do it alone. Your support is essential in keeping our team locked in helping Christian organize for January 31st. Your support sent David and our organizers to Houston to knock on doors before the primary and pushed Christian to first place in a 16-way field.
**But runoff elections are won on turnout and momentum — and this one is too close for comfort.** His runoff opponent finished _right behind him_ in the primary and is nearly matching his fundraising. Even a brief slowdown in fundraising could mean the difference in winning a tight race and put everything we’ve built at risk.
Still not convinced your support matters? Look no further than Houston, just last year. Because of grassroots supporters like you, Leaders We Deserve went all out to help elect ER nurse Molly Cook to the Texas Senate — and **she won by _just 74 votes._** Plus, that victory strengthened the very organizing infrastructure that helped lift Christian into this runoff, and his win could fuel the next young Texas progressive, and the next.
**Because this is about so much more than one race — this is how we build _long-term_ , youth-led progressive power with a track record of taking on Trump and winning.** And it’s why it’s so crucial we hit this fundraising deadline by midnight tomorrow, keep our momentum alive, and win this runoff — and time is running out to do it.
[**Peter, will you split a contribution today to help Christian Menefee win and invest in a new generation of young progressive leaders?** ](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251129_1_menefee.aSZvYS&refcode=em20251129-2886&amount=5&t=4&akid=2886%2E587540%2EgUMyNI)
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251129_1_menefee.aSZvYS&amount=5&refcode=em20251129-2886&t=23&akid=2886%2E587540%2EgUMyNI)**
Thank you for helping us win this runoff, send Christian to Congress, and build long-term generational power.
— Leaders We Deserve
P.S. When David was on the ground in Houston before Christian’s primary, he met Christian’s young son at a canvass launch. His son introduced himself to the volunteers just like his dad: “I’m Christian Menefee, and I’m running for Congress.” It was adorable — but also a reminder of what we’re fighting for, Peter. [**If you believe in this mission, will you split a contribution of $5 today?**](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251129_1_menefee.aSZvYS&refcode=em20251129-2886&amount=5&t=24&akid=2886%2E587540%2EgUMyNI)
[](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251129_1_menefee.aSZvYS&refcode=em20251129-2886&t=25&akid=2886%2E587540%2EgUMyNI)
* * *

clean_body: Your support will help elect a proven fighter for his community and invest in long-term progressive power. Hi Peter. The countdown is on in Houston: Christian Menefee’s runoff is officially scheduled for January 31st — a narrow window to finish what we started and elect a young progressive who will champion his community and fight corporate power in Washington. But the timing couldn’t be tougher: our end-of-month deadline hits TOMORROW at midnight , and holiday giving dropped off right when we needed to surge. We’re inches away from winning Christian’s runoff, electing Akbar Ali in Georgia, and ramping up for 2026 — we cannot afford to lose momentum now. Christian has never backed down. As Harris County Attorney, he’s taken on major corporations and President Trump for targeting his community — and he’s beaten them again and again. Now he’s ready to take that fight to Congress for universal healthcare, affordability, and everyday families. But he can’t do it alone. Your support is essential in keeping our team locked in helping Christian organize for January 31st. Your support sent David and our organizers to Houston to knock on doors before the primary and pushed Christian to first place in a 16-way field. But runoff elections are won on turnout and momentum — and this one is too close for comfort. His runoff opponent finished right behind him in the primary and is nearly matching his fundraising. Even a brief slowdown in fundraising could mean the difference in winning a tight race and put everything we’ve built at risk. Still not convinced your support matters? Look no further than Houston, just last year. Because of grassroots supporters like you, Leaders We Deserve went all out to help elect ER nurse Molly Cook to the Texas Senate — and she won by just 74 votes. Plus, that victory strengthened the very organizing infrastructure that helped lift Christian into this runoff, and his win could fuel the next young Texas progressive, and the next. Because this is about so much more than one race — this is how we build long-term , youth-led progressive power with a track record of taking on Trump and winning. And it’s why it’s so crucial we hit this fundraising deadline by midnight tomorrow, keep our momentum alive, and win this runoff — and time is running out to do it. Thank you for helping us win this runoff, send Christian to Congress, and build long-term generational power. — Leaders We Deserve P.S. When David was on the ground in Houston before Christian’s primary, he met Christian’s young son at a canvass launch. His son introduced himself to the volunteers just like his dad: “I’m Christian Menefee, and I’m running for Congress.” It was adorable — but also a reminder of what we’re fighting for, Peter.

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | A self-driving Tesla drifting down an empty road...
